Name: Liquid Rocket Lab (LRL)
Relevance: A student-run facility dedicated to the development and testing of liquid-fueled rocket engines and orbital-class rockets.

Name: Astronautics Laboratory
Relevance: Focuses on spacecraft system testing, including hardware-in-the-loop and software verification for CubeSats.

Name: UAV Lab
Relevance: Equipped with over 40 UAVs, focusing on autonomous flight, obstacle avoidance, and multidisciplinary design optimization.

Name: Small Satellite (SmallSat) Laboratory
Relevance: Conducts research on small satellite technologies, ADCS hardware-in-the-loop testbeds, and the S-Kubed research group.

Name: CubeSat Technology Exploration Program (CubeSTEP)
Relevance: An interdisciplinary program for testing and launching CubeSats, with a planned launch for its latest spacecraft in 2027.

Name: S-Kubed (Small Satellite Systems & Software)
Relevance: Research group focused on custom control and data-handling frameworks for small satellites and ADCS development.

Name: Bronco Space
Relevance: A major student organization dedicated to CubeSat missions, hardware development, and hands-on space technology projects.

Name: CPP Rocketry / URC (University Rocketry Club)
Relevance: Student team that designs, builds, and launches high-powered rockets, competing in international competitions like FAR-OUT.

Name: Space and Satellite Student Association (SSSA)
Relevance: Organization focused on satellite technology education and networking within the aerospace industry.

Name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) CPP Chapter
Relevance: The local chapter of the professional society for aerospace engineers, hosting design competitions and professional development.

Name: Cal Poly Pomona Robotics Club
Relevance: Develops robotic systems that are often applicable to planetary exploration and autonomous space operations.

Name: Formula SAE (High Altitude/Propulsion adjacency)
Relevance: While automotive, it involves advanced composites and internal combustion/electric propulsion knowledge adjacent to aerospace systems.

Name: Cal Poly Pomona Observatory
Relevance: Facility used for astronomical research and student training in observational techniques and data analysis.

Name: Society of Physics Students (SPS)
Relevance: Engages in physics research and outreach, often covering astrophysics and planetary science topics.

Name: AECOX Team (Historical)
Relevance: A historical NASA SEED campaign team that flew a microgravity experiment on atmospheric reentry control.

Name: NASA SEED Teams
Relevance: Various interdisciplinary teams that have historically participated in NASA’s reduced gravity flights and systems engineering challenges.

Name: Mechanical Engineering Design Teams
Relevance: Teams working on autonomous vehicles and sensors that provide foundational tech for space robotics.

Name: Planetary Science Group (Physics & Astronomy Dept)
Relevance: Faculty and students conducting research into planetary surfaces and solar system evolution.

Name: Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) Group
Relevance: Analyzes high-speed flows and heat transfer relevant to rocket nozzles and aerospace vehicle design.

Name: Aerostructures and Composites Lab
Relevance: Focuses on the materials and structural integrity of aerospace components, including satellites and UAVs.
